title: Pre-expansion controller protection phase
summary: After the docs and home-place wave, lane 1 should keep buying down big-file risk with one focused route-notice split and one focused field-season-board composition split before another major chapter wave lands.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the next route-notice or feedback-state split handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The biggest coordinators are still large enough to make future chapter work risky.

execution_order.goal: Implement the next route-notice or feedback-state split.
execution_order.why_now: One focused extraction can keep feel work and route logic from piling into game.ts and overlay-render.ts.

execution_order.goal: Review the next route-notice or feedback-state split.
execution_order.why_now: Structural cleanup only helps if it stays behavior-safe.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the field-season-board composition split handoff.
execution_order.why_now: field-season-board.ts is now big enough to deserve its own focused protection pass.
Board-state composition should keep getting smaller before the next chapter wave.
The shell should stay stable while the risk surface shrinks.

guardrails: Do not redesign the product during the split.
Preserve save behavior, route state, and station shell behavior.
Prefer pure derivation helpers and composition extractors over broad abstraction.

main_254_focus.target_seam: route notice, notice variant, or feedback-state derivation
recommended_shape: Extract a pure field-notice descriptor helper for filed-route and notebook-ready notices.
Keep showFieldNotice(), timer mutation, enqueue ordering, and save writes in game.ts.
Do not widen the split into the guided maybeShow* notice family yet.
Extract the pure season-wrap and subtitle composition family into a dedicated field-season-wrap helper module.
Keep route-state, replay-note, atlas, archive, expedition, and next-season locator logic in field-season-board.ts.
Retarget field-station-state.ts to the new wrap helper directly, using type-only imports to avoid runtime cycles.
